items.title: U.K. military deployed to deter threat to undersea cables amid Russian sub presence
items.summary_auto: Britain deployed military vessels to prevent any attacks on cables and pipelines by Russian submarines that spent more than a month ‌in and around British waters earlier this year, Defence Minister John Healey said on Thursday.

items.title: Caufield scores 50th goal, becomes 1st Hab to reach milestone in more than 35 years
items.summary_auto: Cole Caufield scored his 50th goal of the season on Thursday evening in the Montreal Canadiens' game versus the Tampa Bay Lightning, becoming the first Habs player to reach that milestone since Stéphane Richer did it during the 1989-90 season.

items.title: Poilievre challenges PM Carney to defend private property rights in B.C. in wake of Cowichan decision
items.summary_auto: Federal Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is calling on Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal government to defend private property rights in face of what he claims is a threat posed by a landmark B.C. Supreme Court decision establishing Aboriginal title on private property.

items.title: Environment Canada to use AI in new weather forecasting model
items.summary_auto: Environment and Climate Change Canada will use artificial intelligence to make its weather forecasts more accurate, the federal department said Thursday.

items.title: Artemis II in photos: around the moon and inside Orion
items.summary_auto: The dozens of photos taken by the astronauts include never before seen views of the moon, a total solar eclipse and a stunning shot of Earth.

items.title: Putin claims Russia will enact 32-hour ceasefire over Orthodox Easter weekend
items.summary_auto: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Thursday declared a 32-hour ceasefire in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter weekend, following an earlier call from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for a pause in some of the hostilities to observe the holiday.

items.title: Trump rages, NATO endures: Why the alliance is harder to kill than it looks
items.summary_auto: Despite threats from U.S. President Donald Trump, an American break with NATO remains unlikely. Political constraints, military dependence and mutual interests bind both sides. The real danger involves eroding trust, emboldening Russia and a slow-motion fracture that weakens deterrence without ever triggering a formal divorce.

items.title: Emperor penguins now endangered as climate change shrinks sea ice
items.summary_auto: Emperor penguins, the largest penguin species, are now listed as endangered following a new international assessment of how climate change is affecting their habitat and population.

items.title: Health Canada warns against injecting unauthorized drugs promising anti-aging, weight loss or wellness
items.summary_auto: People shouldn’t buy or use unauthorized, injectable peptide drugs claiming to help with body issues and wellness, warns Health Canada — they can come with serious side effects.

items.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein in surprise statement at White House
items.summary_auto: First lady Melania Trump is denying ties to Jeffrey Epstein and knowledge of his crimes, saying Thursday that the "stories are completely false" and calling accusations that she was somehow involved "smears about me."

items.title: Port expansion near Montreal kicks off as 1st 'nation-building' project
items.summary_auto: Construction has officially started on a port expansion near Montreal — the first so-called nation-building project that was fast-tracked for approval by the federal government.

items.title: Fake maple syrup plot thickens: Cans found with label hiding name of implicated company
These B.C. students are trying to drum up $250K to save their music programs
items.summary_auto: For a trio of B.C. high school band students, the idea came to them, clear as a bell: if no one else would step in to save their school’s music programs — they would.

items.title: The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals her real name
items.summary_auto: It turns out the biggest twist in Freida McFadden’s career isn’t in her books. The bestselling author, known for keeping readers guessing, is revealing a surprise of her own: Her real name is Sara Cohen.

items.title: Former chief Crown attorney sues N.S. alleging bullying, unjust demotion in lawsuit
items.summary_auto: A former chief Crown attorney is suing the Nova Scotia government and the province’s Public Prosecution Service, alleging she was bullied, sidelined without explanation and ultimately forced out of her leadership role.

items.title: Soccer fans give 'stupid, stupid' 2026 World Cup jerseys the cold shoulder
items.summary_auto: Fans love the designs of Nike's new World Cup soccer jerseys. They do not love the way they bunch at the shoulders.

items.title: Gas flaring at LNG Canada far exceeds permitted volume, documents show
items.summary_auto: Documents show the volume of gas flared at the LNG Canada plant on B.C. 's North Coast between October and January far surpassed what its permit allows.

items.title: Legal arguments wrap up in Stronach sex assault trial, possible verdict in June
items.summary_auto: Legal arguments in the sexual assault trial of Canadian businessman Frank Stronach wrapped up on Thursday with a verdict possibly coming in mid-June.

items.title: Manitoba requests military help to prepare Peguis First Nation for major spring flood
items.summary_auto: The Canadian military has been asked to help prepare Manitoba's largest First Nation community for an expected spring flood that could reach devastating levels.

items.title: Air Canada is testing a new program to resolve customer complaints. Will it work?
Appeals court judges question whether four-year prison sentence for Sean 'Diddy' Combs was too harsh
items.summary_auto: A three-judge panel in Manhattan weighed the "exceptionally difficult case" in court on Thursday, and did not immediately rule after hearing two hours of arguments. Combs, who was convicted last July under the federal Mann Act which bans transporting people across state lines for any sexual crime, is challenging his conviction and prison sentence.

items.title: K-pop supergroup BTS kick off world tour in South Korea after 4-year hiatus
items.summary_auto: The boys of BTS are returning to the stage after a nearly four-year hiatus, during which the members completed their mandatory military service. Despite pouring rain in Seoul, fans packed a stadium to watch the K-pop group kick off their long-awaited world tour.

items.title: She travelled 900 km for an abortion. Years later, Quebec access issues remain urgent
Netanyahu approves direct talks with Lebanon but vows to keep striking Hezbollah
items.summary_auto: Israel bombed more targets in Lebanon on Thursday, putting the Middle East ceasefire in further jeopardy after its most intensive strikes of the war on its neighbour killed more than 300 people, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry.

items.title: Heather Winterstein shouldn’t have been in wait room, needed frequent checks, medical expert tells inquest
items.summary_auto: On the day she died of sepsis, Heather Winterstein shouldn't have been placed in the emergency room waiting area at the St. Catharines hospital until she could see a doctor, an emergency medicine expert told a coroner's inquest Wednesday.

items.title: Alberta government faces another legal fight to keep supervised consumption sites open
items.summary_auto: Less than a year after a judge ruled on a similar case in Red Deer, the provincial government is facing an injunction seeking to stop supervised consumption services from closing in Calgary and Lethbridge.

items.title: Award-winning documentary Agatha's Almanac captures 92-year-old's life on ancestral Manitoba farm
items.summary_auto: Amalie Atkins's award-winning documentary offers a glimpse into the colour and texture of the life of her 92-year-old aunt, Agatha Bock, as she works an ancestral farm in southern Manitoba.

items.title: Trump casts doubt on ceasefire as Iran maintains chokehold on Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: The Middle East ceasefire appeared shaky less than two days after it was announced by U.S. President Donald Trump, as Israel bombed more targets in Lebanon on Thursday and Iran showed little sign of relinquishing its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Marilyn Gladu says she's pro-choice, anti conversion therapy as Liberals embrace former Conservative
items.summary_auto: Once seen as a social conservative, floor-crosser Marilyn Gladu clarified some of her previous positions from the sidelines of the Liberal convention Thursday, saying she's pro-choice and anti conversion therapy.

items.title: You can now get a prescription for nature access if you live in Hamilton, here's how
items.summary_auto: The PaRx program, an initiative that allows healthcare providers to prescribe nature passes to patients, is now available for Hamilton Conservation Authority (HCA) sites.

items.title: Trump and Iran remain far apart on peace terms. Here are the biggest gaps
items.summary_auto: Despite U.S. President Donald Trump's sunny assessment of the prospects for a long-term peace agreement with Iran, there are fundamental differences in the visions the two sides have put forward for a deal.

items.title: Why human eyes are better at observing the moon than cameras
items.summary_auto: Human eyes are a wonder of nature. While we've invented the camera to work like our eyes, it's not as good as the real thing, something that was highlighted during Artemis II's lunar flyby.
